info.name: InsightXpert API
info.description:
API collection for InsightXpert — AI data analyst for Indian digital payment transactions.

Base URL: http://localhost:8000

**Authentication:** Most endpoints require a valid session cookie. Call `Auth > Login` first — Postman will automatically store the `__session` httpOnly cookie and send it with subsequent requests.

**Admin endpoints** require a session from a user with `is_admin=true` in the database.

variable.description: Set to a conversation ID from List Conversations
Set to a dataset ID from List Datasets

item.name: Auth
item.description: Authentication endpoints. Login first to get a session cookie, which is required by most other endpoints.

request.description:
Authenticate with email and password. Returns user info and sets an httpOnly `__session` cookie.

Postman will automatically store the cookie and send it with subsequent requests.
Clear the session cookie and log out.
Returns the currently authenticated user. Requires a valid session cookie.
Returns server status and timestamp. Use this to verify the server is running. No authentication required.

Send a natural language question and receive Server-Sent Events (SSE) stream.

`agent_mode` values: `"auto"` (default), `"analyst"`, `"statistician"`.

Chunk types in the stream:
- `status` — progress update (e.g., "Searching knowledge base...")
- `tool_call` — agent is calling a tool (run_sql, get_schema, search_similar)
- `sql` — the SQL query being executed
- `tool_result` — result from tool execution
- `answer` — final natural language answer
- `error` — error message
- `metrics` — token usage and timing: `{"input_tokens": 1234, "output_tokens": 567, "generation_time_ms": 4200}`

Stream ends with `data: [DONE]`.

**Note:** Postman does not render SSE streams well. Use curl for best results:
```
curl -N -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/chat \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -b '__session=YOUR_SESSION' \
  -d '{"message":"What is the average transaction amount?","agent_mode":"auto","skip_clarification":false}'
```

response.name: 200 SSE Stream

request.description:
Send a question and get all response chunks at once (non-streaming).

Returns a JSON object with a `chunks` array containing all the same chunk types as the SSE endpoint, but collected into a single response. Better for Postman testing since it returns normal JSON.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Run the full pipeline and return only the final answer text, conversation ID, and any SQL that was executed.

Use this when you only need the answer string and don't care about intermediate status/tool chunks.

Response fields:
- `answer` — the final natural language answer
- `conversation_id` — use this to continue the conversation in subsequent requests
- `sql` — list of SQL queries executed (may be empty for non-SQL questions)

response.name: 200 OK

item.name: Chat — Example Questions
item.description: Pre-built requests for each of the 6 query categories in the challenge.

item.name: Comparative — Avg amount by txn type
Conversations
item.description: CRUD operations for persistent conversation history.

request.description: List all conversations for the authenticated user, ordered by most recently updated.

response.name: 200 OK

query.description: Search query (minimum 2 characters). Searches conversation titles and message content.

request.description: Search across conversation titles and message content. Returns matching conversations with message snippets.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Get a single conversation with all its messages and chunk data.

Set the `{{conversation_id}}` collection variable to an actual conversation ID from the List endpoint.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: Rename a conversation. Set `{{conversation_id}}` to the target conversation ID.

Star or unstar a conversation. Set `starred` to `false` to unstar.

Set `{{conversation_id}}` to the target conversation ID.
Permanently delete a conversation and all its messages. Set `{{conversation_id}}` to the target conversation ID.
Returns the full DDL (CREATE TABLE statements) and list of table names from the connected database.

response.name: 200 OK

item.name: SQL
item.description: Execute raw SQL queries against the database (read-only).

request.description:
Execute a read-only SQL query against the database. No authentication required.

Only SELECT, WITH, and EXPLAIN queries are allowed. INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, DROP, ALTER, CREATE, and other write operations are blocked (403).

Results are capped at 1000 rows with a 30-second timeout.

response.name: 403 Forbidden — Write blocked

item.name: Config
item.description: View and switch the LLM provider/model at runtime.

request.description:
Returns the current LLM provider, model, and all available providers with their models.

Ollama models are only listed if the Ollama server is reachable.

Switch the active LLM provider and model at runtime. Takes effect immediately for all subsequent chat requests.

Available Gemini models: gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash-lite, gemini-2.0-flash, gemini-2.0-flash-lite.

Switch to a local Ollama model. The model must already be downloaded (use the Ollama > Pull endpoint first).

Returns 503 if Ollama is not running or the model is not found.

response.name: 503 Ollama unreachable

request.description:
Add a question-SQL pair to the RAG knowledge base. The agent uses these as few-shot examples for future queries.

`type` can be: `qa_pair`, `ddl`, or `documentation`.
Add business documentation to the RAG store. This context is injected into the agent's system prompt when relevant.
Add DDL schema information to the RAG store.

Delete all RAG embeddings from all ChromaDB collections (qa_pairs, ddl, docs, findings).

This is a destructive operation — the RAG knowledge base will be empty after this call. The server will re-bootstrap training data on next restart.

Requires authentication.

item.name: Feedback
item.description: Submit thumbs-up/down feedback on assistant messages.

request.description:
Submit feedback on an assistant message.

- `message_id`: ID of the assistant message to rate
- `feedback`: `true` = thumbs up, `false` = thumbs down, `null` = clear existing feedback
- `comment`: optional free-text feedback

item.name: Ollama
item.description:
Manage Ollama models — pull (download), list, and delete local models.

Requires Ollama to be running on the server (default: http://localhost:11434).

request.description:
Download the Ministral 3B model from the Ollama registry. Streams progress via SSE.

SSE events include:
- `{"status": "pulling manifest"}` — starting download
- `{"status": "downloading ...", "completed": 1234, "total": 5678, "percent": 21.7}` — download progress
- `{"status": "success", "model": "ministral:3b"}` — download complete
- `[DONE]` — stream end

**Note:** Use curl for best SSE experience:
```
curl -N -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/ollama/pull \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"model":"ministral:3b"}'
```

Download the Llama 3.2 1B model. Streams progress via SSE.

Llama 3.2 comes in 1B and 3B variants (no 2B). Use `llama3.2:3b` for the larger version.
Download the Llama 3.2 3B model. Streams progress via SSE.
Pull any Ollama model by name. Replace the model value with any model from the Ollama registry (https://ollama.com/library).
List all Ollama models that are currently downloaded on the server.

Delete a locally downloaded Ollama model to free disk space.

Replace `ministral:3b` in the URL with the model name to delete.

item.name: Client Config
item.description: Retrieve the resolved feature configuration for the current authenticated user.

request.description:
Returns the resolved feature configuration for the current authenticated user based on their org membership.

Admin users receive `{"config": null, "is_admin": true, "org_id": null}` since they bypass feature restrictions.

response.name: 200 OK — Regular user

item.name: Admin — Config
item.description:
Admin-only endpoints for managing global and per-organization client configuration.

Requires admin authentication.

request.description:
Get the full client configuration including admin domains, user-org mappings, organizations, and defaults.

Requires admin authentication.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Update the global client configuration including admin domains, user-org mappings, and default feature flags.

Requires admin authentication.

List all configured organizations.

Requires admin authentication.

Get the feature configuration for a specific organization. Replace `org-001` in the path with the target org ID.

Requires admin authentication.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Create or update the feature configuration for a specific organization. Replace `org-001` in the path with the target org ID.

Requires admin authentication.

Delete the configuration for a specific organization. The org's users will fall back to the global defaults. Replace `org-001` in the path with the target org ID.

Requires admin authentication.

item.name: Admin — Users & Conversations
item.description:
Admin-only endpoints for viewing and managing users and their conversations.

Requires admin authentication.

request.description:
List all registered users with usage statistics.

Requires admin authentication.

List all conversations for a specific user. No ownership check — admin can view any user's conversations. Replace `user-123` in the path with the target user ID.

Requires admin authentication.

Get the full conversation including all messages. No ownership check — admin can retrieve any conversation.

Requires admin authentication.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Delete a single conversation by ID. No ownership check — admin can delete any conversation.

Requires admin authentication.

Delete all conversations belonging to a specific user. Replace `user-123` in the path with the target user ID.

Requires admin authentication.

DESTRUCTIVE — deletes all conversations across all users. This cannot be undone.

Requires admin authentication.

item.name: Admin — RAG
item.description:
Admin-only endpoints for managing RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) knowledge base content.

Requires admin authentication.

request.description:
Delete only QA pairs from the RAG knowledge base. DDL schemas, documentation, and findings are kept intact.

Requires admin authentication.

item.name: Admin — Prompts
item.description:
Admin-only endpoints for managing agent system prompt templates.

Requires admin authentication.

request.description:
List all stored prompt templates. Templates stored here override the file-based defaults.

Requires admin authentication.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Get a single prompt template by name. Replace `analyst` in the path with the target prompt name.

Requires admin authentication.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "id": "prompt-uuid-here",
  "name": "analyst",
  "content": "You are an expert SQL analyst...",
  "description": "Analyst agent prompt",
  "is_active": true,
  "created_at": "2026-02-01T00:00:00",
  "updated_at": "2026-02-15T10:00:00"
}

request.description:
Create or update a named prompt template. Stored prompts override the file-based defaults. Replace `analyst` in the path with the target prompt name.

Requires admin authentication.

Delete a stored prompt template. The agent will revert to the file-based default for that prompt name. Replace `analyst` in the path with the target prompt name.

Requires admin authentication.

Reset a prompt template to the file-based default, discarding any DB-stored override. Replace `analyst` in the path with the target prompt name.

Requires admin authentication.

item.name: Datasets
item.description:
Manage datasets and their metadata. Most endpoints require admin authentication. `GET /api/datasets/public` is accessible to any authenticated user.

Set the `{{dataset_id}}` collection variable to a dataset ID from the List Datasets response.

request.description:
List all datasets with full metadata.

Requires admin authentication.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: List available datasets with minimal public information. Accessible to any authenticated user.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Get full dataset details including column metadata and example queries.

Requires admin authentication.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "id": "dataset-uuid-here",
  "name": "Indian Payments 2024",
  "description": "250K synthetic Indian digital payment transactions",
  "ddl": "CREATE TABLE transactions (...);",
  "documentation": "This dataset contains...",
  "is_active": true,
  "table_name": "transactions",
  "columns": [
    {
      "id": "col-uuid-here",
      "column_name": "amount_inr",
      "column_type": "REAL",
      "description": "Transaction amount in Indian Rupees",
      "domain_values": null,
      "domain_rules": "Always positive. Range: 1 to 100000.",
      "ordinal_position": 5
    }
  ],
  "example_queries": [
    {
      "id": "query-uuid-here",
      "question": "What is the most common transaction type?",
      "sql": "SELECT transaction_type, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM transactions GROUP BY transaction_type ORDER BY cnt DESC LIMIT 1;",
      "category": "descriptive"
    }
  ],
  "created_at": "2026-01-01T00:00:00",
  "updated_at": "2026-02-15T10:00:00"
}

request.description:
Update dataset fields. All fields are optional — only include the fields you want to change.

Requires admin authentication.

Set this dataset as the active dataset. Only one dataset can be active at a time.

Requires admin authentication.

body.raw:
{
  "column_name": "amount_inr",
  "column_type": "REAL",
  "description": "Transaction amount in Indian Rupees",
  "domain_values": null,
  "domain_rules": "Always positive. Range: 1 to 100000.",
  "ordinal_position": 5
}

request.description:
Add column-level metadata to a dataset. This metadata is used to enrich RAG context for the agent.

Requires admin authentication.

body.raw:
{
  "column_name": "amount_inr",
  "column_type": "REAL",
  "description": "Transaction amount in Indian Rupees",
  "domain_values": null,
  "domain_rules": "Always positive. Range: 1 to 100000.",
  "ordinal_position": 5
}

request.description:
Update an existing column metadata entry. Replace `col-uuid-here` in the path with the column record ID.

Requires admin authentication.

Add an example question-SQL pair to the dataset. Example queries are used during RAG training.

Requires admin authentication.

Delete an example query from the dataset. Replace `query-uuid-here` in the path with the query record ID.

Requires admin authentication.

Re-run RAG training using all metadata stored in the dataset (DDL, documentation, column descriptions, and example queries). Existing embeddings for this dataset are replaced.

Requires admin authentication.
